app.name: NTM (Named Tmux Manager)
app.one_liner: NTM transforms tmux into a durable, policy-aware control plane for orchestrating multiple AI coding agents with TUI dashboards, work triage, safety guards, and automation APIs.

screens.name: Command Palette
screens.purpose: Provide searchable access to all NTM commands including session management, work assignment, safety controls, and automation triggers.

screens.name: Dashboard
screens.purpose: Visualize active agent sessions, work status, mail inbox, and safety alerts in a TUI interface.

rust_commands.name: ntm_checkpoint
rust_commands.purpose: Capture durable state snapshots including session layout, agent outputs, timelines, and audit logs for replayability.

rust_commands.name: ntm_mail
rust_commands.purpose: Manage human-agent communication via an inbox system that supports replies, assignments, and approvals.

rust_commands.name: ntm_safety_approve
rust_commands.purpose: Enforce destructive operation policies by requiring explicit approval workflows before execution.

rust_commands.name: ntm_send
rust_commands.purpose: Deliver work items, interrupts, or follow-ups to specific agent panes using structured messaging and prompt injection.

rust_commands.name: ntm_serve_api
rust_commands.purpose: Expose REST, WebSocket, and SSE endpoints alongside OpenAPI spec generation for external tool integration.

rust_commands.name: ntm_spawn
rust_commands.purpose: Launch a new tmux session with a specified AI agent type (e.g., Claude, Cursor, Ollama), applying configuration, prompts, and pane labeling.

rust_commands.name: ntm_work_triaage
rust_commands.purpose: Analyze codebase graph to recommend next actionable tasks, considering impact, dependencies, and agent capabilities.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add Agent Mail inbox view
week 1: Build basic command palette with search
week 1: Implement core tmux session spawning with agent labeling
week 1: Integrate simple safety guard for rm -rf patterns
week 2: Add graph-aware work triage engine
week 2: Expose REST API with OpenAPI spec
week 2: Implement checkpointing with timeline capture
week 2: Support multi-agent coordination via file reservations
acceptance_tests: AGENTS.md is generated during project init even when aliases exist [E-IS-002]
Memory limits apply correctly in containerized environments without systemd [E-IS-006]
Multi-line prompts are correctly submitted to agents without newline stripping [E-IS-007]
REST API serves valid OpenAPI spec and accepts WebSocket connections [E-RD-007]
Safety policy blocks destructive commands unless explicitly approved [E-RD-006]
User can spawn a Claude Code agent session via 'ntm spawn claude' and see it labeled in dashboard [E-RL-002]
